channel.title: Morning Tax
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Tax examines the latest news in tax politics and policy.

item.title: Settlement controversy ensnares tax administration legislation
item.description: The fallout from President Donald Trump’s IRS settlement continues to reverberate.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Trying to close off a runway for taxing the rich
item.description: America’s tax-the-rich debate has reached the friendly skies.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Never-ending fund
item.description: Congress might be out of town this week, but it doesn’t look like lawmakers will be forgetting about President Donald Trump’s IRS settlement any time soon.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Judging adversaries
item.description: A new court filing helps explain why President Donald Trump and the government that he runs might be rushing toward a legal settlement.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Plotting the comeback
item.description: Rep. Richard Neal (D-Mass.) is making plans in case he gets to take the gavel of the House Ways and Means Committee for a second time.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Counting up carried interest
item.description: A new analysis has found that curbing the preferential treatment of carried interest might raise more revenue than previously estimated.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Cross-Atlantic DST tensions land in Washington
item.description: The United Kingdom’s digital services tax could present some awkward moments during King Charles III’s visit to the United States this week.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker

item.title: Time to ask for more of an overtime deduction
item.description: Two dozen labor groups are pushing lawmakers to expand the new deduction for overtime pay, which proved to be a big hit with taxpayers this filing season.
item.dc:creator: By Bernie Becker
